customer_anxiety_profile: Dave is a cooperative, curious existing customer — not anxious in the traditional sense. His hesitation is cost-based ('batteries ain't cheap') and informational ('just to see what I would be getting into'). He is not a skeptic or a hard close; he is a cautious evaluator who needs the closer to answer his production question AND make the battery ROI case in terms of his specific ComEd situation.
unresolved_concerns: Why doesn't his system production percentage go lower on sunny days — Dave's original question was never fully resolved technically. The rep routed it to the closer, but the closer should be prepared to answer it clearly or the call starts on the back foot.
Whether a battery is actually worth the cost for his specific situation — Dave asked this directly and received a generic explanation, not a personalized answer.
sensitive_topics_raised: Cost anxiety on battery storage — Dave volunteered 'batteries ain't cheap' unprompted. The closer should not lead with price.
ComEd buyback rate gap — the rep introduced the 70% buyback frame; the closer should be prepared to quantify this in Dave's specific situation if possible.
exact_promises_rep_made: Callback on Tuesday at 11AM from the closer ([Tate / Kate — name unclear from transcript; DATA QUALITY FLAG: rep said 'Kate' at 07:36 and 'Tay' at 08:09 and 08:51 — confirm the closer's actual name before the call so Dave isn't confused when someone introduces themselves differently]).
The closer will walk Dave through what a battery would look like and whether it makes sense for his situation.
The closer will also be able to address Dave's production percentage question.
closer_should_know: Dave trimmed a tree to improve his system's production — he is an engaged, invested customer who wants the system to perform. That's the anchor for the battery pitch: he already took action to produce more; a battery means he keeps what he produces instead of giving it to ComEd at a discount. Lead with that frame, not with product specs. Also: no text confirmation was sent after the call — this callback was booked over a weekend with no follow-up artifact. Confirm Dave is still expecting the call before dialing.
